summary: Completed testing of Service Desk improvements. All requested features verified: Reply Modal with Internal Note/Email/WhatsApp buttons, Templates and AI Draft buttons, Message textarea, Cancel and Send buttons. Backend APIs for messaging/send and ai/draft-reply working correctly. Fixed route ordering issue for canned-responses endpoint.

service_desk_loads.details: Service Desk loads at /admin/service-desk with 277+ tickets displayed

admin_login.details: Login with admin credentials (aditya/lola4304) works correctly

ticket_list_display.details: Ticket list displays correctly with ticket IDs, member names, descriptions, status badges

ticket_details_panel.details: Clicking on a ticket shows ticket details panel with Member Details, Customer History, Assignment, Activity Timeline, Conversation sections

reply_to_ticket_button.details: Reply to Ticket button exists and opens Reply Modal

reply_modal_internal_note.details: Internal Note button works in Reply Modal, shows warning about internal-only visibility

reply_modal_email.details: Email button shows email address in modal, disabled if no email available

reply_modal_whatsapp.details: WhatsApp button works in Reply Modal, shows phone number, opens wa.me URL

reply_modal_templates.details: Templates button shows dropdown with canned responses (8 default templates)

reply_modal_ai_draft.details: AI Draft button shows dropdown with Professional, Friendly & Warm, Empathetic, Quick Response options

reply_modal_textarea.details: Message textarea present with placeholder text based on selected channel

reply_modal_cancel_send.details: Cancel and Send buttons present, Send button text changes based on channel (Add Note/Send Email/Open WhatsApp)

internal_notes_display.details: Internal notes display with yellow 'Internal Note' badge in conversation section

merge_tickets_modal.details: Merge option found in ticket context menu, opens Merge Tickets modal

backend_messaging_send.details: POST /api/tickets/messaging/send works for internal, email, and whatsapp channels

backend_canned_responses.details: GET /api/tickets/canned-responses returns 8 default templates (fixed route ordering issue)

critical_code_review_comments: Fixed route ordering issue: moved canned-responses routes before /{ticket_id} route to prevent route conflicts
Reply Modal implementation is comprehensive with all required features
Email sending uses Resend API with proper HTML templates
WhatsApp integration uses wa.me URL for click-to-chat functionality
AI Draft uses EMERGENT_LLM_KEY for GPT-4o integration
context_for_next_testing_agent: Service Desk Reply Modal fully functional with Internal Note, Email, WhatsApp channels. Templates dropdown shows 8 default canned responses. AI Draft generates professional replies using GPT-4o. Internal notes display with yellow badge in conversation. Merge Tickets modal accessible via ticket context menu. Fixed canned-responses route ordering issue.

verified_features.reply_modal: VERIFIED - Opens with ticket summary, reply type selection, templates, AI draft, message textarea, action buttons
verified_features.internal_note: VERIFIED - Yellow badge displayed in conversation, warning shown in modal
verified_features.email_sending: VERIFIED - Uses Resend API, shows recipient email in modal
verified_features.whatsapp_sending: VERIFIED - Opens wa.me URL in new tab with pre-filled message
verified_features.ai_draft: VERIFIED - Generates professional replies using GPT-4o via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY
verified_features.merge_tickets: VERIFIED - Modal accessible via context menu, shows selected tickets

bug_fixes_applied.canned_responses_route: Fixed route ordering - moved /canned-responses before /{ticket_id} to prevent 'Ticket not found' error
